message:
✨ Implement Mission Control dry-run mode (#4229)

Wire the existing "Dry Run" button stub to perform server-side
validation without creating actual resources:

- types.ts: Add isDryRun to MissionControlState
- useMissionControl.ts: Add setDryRun action, persist via localStorage
- useMissions.tsx: Add dryRun to StartMissionParams, inject
  --dry-run=server (kubectl) and --dry-run (helm) into prompts
- LaunchSequence.tsx: Pass dryRun flag, prefix titles with [DRY RUN],
  update completion headers
- MissionControlDialog.tsx: Wire Dry Run button (setDryRun + launch),
  clear isDryRun on real deploy, add yellow DRY RUN badge in header

Uses --dry-run=server (not =client) for server-side validation that
catches missing CRDs, RBAC denials, and quota violations.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:

'Dry-run validation' : 'Automated install'} of ${project.displayName} as part of Mission Control deployment`, type: 'deploy', cluster: clusterName, initialPrompt: prompt + clusterContext, + dryRun: state.isDryRun, }) // Update with missionId @@ -279,9 +281,9 @@ export function LaunchSequence({
